tools.name: identity_get
tools.description: Get your current internet identity: active IPv6, /64 prefix and all addresses in it, tunnel IP, hostname, and IPv4 exit.

tools.name: identity_set_ipv6
tools.description: Set or rotate your public IPv6 address within your /64. Omit address to rotate to a random address (replaces identity_rotate_ipv6). Provide address to pin to a specific one.

address.description: IPv6 address within your /64. Omit to rotate to a random unused address.

tools.name: identity_check_reputation
tools.description: Check if your current IP address is on any spam or abuse blocklists (DNSBL check).

ip.description: IP to check (defaults to your active IPv6)

tools.name: hostname_register
tools.description: Register or update your *.on.route6.me hostname. Creates AAAA + PTR DNS records. Omit name to release (delete) your current hostname.

name.description: Subdomain (e.g. "mybot" → mybot.on.route6.me). Omit to release current hostname.

tools.name: net_ping
tools.description: Ping a host from your Route6 identity. Works for both IPv4 and IPv6 destinations (DNS64 handles IPv4).

tools.name: net_traceroute
tools.description: Traceroute from your Route6 identity to a host.

tools.name: net_dns_resolve
tools.description: Resolve a hostname via DNS64. Shows real AAAA records and synthesized NAT64 addresses for IPv4-only hosts.

tools.name: port_forward_create
tools.description: Expose a host-machine port via your agent's IPv6. scope "public" (default) binds your public address (internet-reachable); scope "mesh" binds only your tunnel address (reachable ONLY by your team mesh at x.mesh.route6.me); "both" creates two listeners. Add ttl_seconds for a temporary forward that auto-expires (max 86400). Omit for persistent.

external_port.description: Public port on your agent IPv6 (1024-65535)

internal_port.description: Port on the host machine (defaults to external_port)

ttl_seconds.description: Auto-expire after N seconds (max 86400). Omit for persistent.

scope.description: Exposure: "public" = internet-reachable on your public IPv6 (default, unchanged behavior); "mesh" = bound only to your tunnel address, reachable only by same-team agents; "both" = two listeners

tools.name: port_forward_list
tools.description: Show all active port forwards with socat status and scope (public / mesh / both).

tools.name: port_forward_delete
tools.description: Remove a port forward and kill the socat process.

tools.name: port_forward_tls
tools.description: Enable or disable Route6-managed TLS termination on a TCP port forward. Enable uses the *.on.route6.me wildcard cert — clients connect with HTTPS, socat decrypts and forwards plain TCP. Requires a registered hostname.

port.description: External port to configure TLS on

tools.name: smtp_allowlist
tools.description: Manage your SMTP allowlist — add, list, or remove mail server destinations. SMTP is blocked by default; allowlisted hosts enable outbound email on port 25/465/587.

action.description: "add" or "remove" an entry, or "list" all entries

address.description: Mail server hostname or email domain (required for add/remove)

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Fetch a URL through your Route6 IPv6 identity. Add screenshot:true to return a base64 PNG screenshot instead of content (paid).

screenshot.description: Return a base64 PNG screenshot instead of content (paid)

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Search the web using Route6 infrastructure via SearXNG.

categories.description: Categories: general, images, news, science, files

tools.name: web_browse
tools.description: Interactive browser session via Playwright. Navigate, click, type, scroll, extract content.

tools.name: scrape
tools.description: Scrape structured content from a URL, or manage scraper credits. Provide url to extract content. Provide action to check balance or purchase credits.

url.description: URL to scrape (mutually exclusive with action)

selector.description: CSS selector for targeted extraction (used with url)

action.description: "balance" to check credits, "topup" to purchase (mutually exclusive with url)

pack.description: Credit pack to purchase (required when action=topup)

tools.name: plan_upgrade
tools.description: Get a Stripe checkout URL to upgrade your Route6 plan.

tools.name: team_status
tools.description: Mesh health summary plus full peer list — peer addresses, hostnames, and online status. Replaces separate mesh_status and mesh_discover.

tools.name: team_ping
tools.description: Ping another agent in your mesh to verify connectivity.

peer.description: Peer tunnel IPv6 address (fd00:baba:deda::XXXX) or hostname

tools.name: team_chat
tools.description: Send or receive broadcast messages to/from all agents in your team mesh. For structured typed work with results, use team_task instead.

action.description: "send" to broadcast a message, "get" to read recent messages

message.description: Chat message to broadcast (required for action=send)

since.description: ISO timestamp — only return messages after this time (action=get)

tools.name: team_whiteboard
tools.description: Read, write, or list the shared team whiteboard — a persistent key-value store for notes, plans, and structured artifacts visible to all team agents. Keys are namespaced: team:<key> (shared), agent:<id>:<key> (private), task:<task_id>:<key> (task-scoped).

action.description: "set" to write, "get" to read, "list" to browse keys

value.description: Value to store (required for set, max 256KB)

supersedes.description: ETag of the version this replaces (set only)

etag.description: Retrieve a specific version by ETag (get only)

tools.name: team_capability
tools.description: Register, renew, list, or deprecate agent capabilities for team task routing. Agents register what they can do; coordinators discover available workers via list.

latency_hint_ms.description: Typical completion time in ms, used for routing (register)

tools.name: team_task
tools.description: Submit, claim, complete, or manage async tasks routed to capable agents. Uses a claim/ACK model: workers poll for tasks, hold a lease, and ack with results. Crashed workers release tasks automatically on claim expiry.

payload.description: Task input, validated against capability input_schema (submit)

claim_ttl_seconds.description: Claim hold time in seconds, default 60 (poll)

max_tasks.description: Max tasks to claim in one call, default 1 (poll)

tools.name: team_events
tools.description: Query the team event log for auditing, debugging, and workflow replay. Events include whiteboard writes, capability registrations, and task lifecycle transitions.

since.description: ISO timestamp start of range (default: last 15 min)

event_type.description: Filter: kv_write, capability_register, capability_expire, task_submit, task_claim, task_complete, task_fail, task_expire, task_cancel

task_id.description: Filter to a specific task full lifecycle

agent_id.description: Filter to events from a specific agent

tools.name: team_metrics
tools.description: Snapshot of team task queue depth, inflight tasks, and per-capability latency and worker stats. Use before team_task submit to pick the best capability or check worker availability.

tools.name: team_loop
tools.description: Enter a continuous receive loop over your team's channels (chat, whiteboard, tasks, project tasks). start → returns a loop_id and protocol instructions; poll → long-polls server-side (~45s) and returns new team activity the moment it happens, plus instructions to handle it and poll again; stop → exit the loop. Lets teammates and other agents continuously push work to you through Route6. Auto-ends after max_idle_cycles consecutive empty polls (default 40 ≈ 30 min at the default hold) or max_duration_seconds (default 7200). status includes stale:true for a loop whose client stopped polling. Pass since_minutes on start to receive recent backlog (e.g. operator messages sent just before the loop started) on the first poll.

action.description: "start" a loop, "poll" for new activity (blocking), "stop" the loop, "status" to list your recent loops

loop_id.description: Loop ID from start (required for poll/stop)

hold_seconds.description: Max server-side block time per poll, default 45. Lower it if your MCP client times out

cursor.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response — pass to re-deliver from that point (poll only, normally omit)

max_idle_cycles.description: Auto-end after this many consecutive empty polls, default 40 (start only)

max_duration_seconds.description: Auto-end after this many seconds total, default 7200 (start only)

since_minutes.description: start only: deliver team activity from the last N minutes as backlog on the first poll (default: loop starts at now)

tools.name: team_project_task
tools.description: Create, update, or list project tasks for your team. Tasks are human-visible with a full lifecycle: pending_approval → open → in_progress → blocked → resolution_proposal → resolved. Agent-submitted tasks require human approval by default (unless auto_approve is on). Use action=create to submit work, action=update to move status or add notes, action=list to see what needs attention.

action.description: "create" a new task or sub-task, "update" status/notes on existing task, "list" tasks by status

description.description: Full description of what needs to be done (create)

parent_task_id.description: Numeric id of parent task to create a sub-task (max 1 level deep)

task_id.description: task_id string from create or list response — required for action=update

status.description: New lifecycle status — agents may set these three values (update)

note.description: Short note recorded in task history (update)

blocked_reason.description: Why progress is blocked — include when status=blocked (update)

resolution_notes.description: What was done to solve the issue — include when status=resolution_proposal (update)

test_results.description: Test output or pass/fail summary proving resolution — include when status=resolution_proposal (update)

status_filter.description: Filter by status (list). Omit to return all non-resolved tasks.

tools.name: team_roles
tools.description: List current role assignments for your team. Use this to discover who is the Project Manager, Code Reviewer, etc. Returns each role with its description and assigned agent hostname (or null if unassigned). Roles are assigned by humans via the web dashboard.
